title: mercurialis perennis subsp. ovata
intro: Mercurialis perennis subsp. ovata is a subspecies of the perennial plant Mercurialis perennis, commonly known as the dog's mercury, a flowering plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae). It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a purgative and diuretic, to treat a range of health conditions. The plant has been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, and is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Mercurialis perennis subsp. ovata, such as tinctures and infusions, are often used to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation in the body.
